title: message_get ids=null: two-query round-trip (query_messages then get_messages)
description: jmap.rs message_get with ids=null calls store.query_messages() to get IDs then store.get_messages(&id_strs) to fetch the rows — two separate DB round-trips for a single logical operation. A single query returning full rows ordered by position would eliminate one RTT. At 256 messages (MAX_GET_ALL) and small SQLite RTTs this is not a problem today, but if the store ever moves to a networked backend or the limit grows, this N+1 structure will hurt. Compare: message_query correctly does a single count + single query. The ids=null path in message_get should be a single store.get_messages_for_channel() call.
